payload.title: Site stabilization needs a governed reconciliation surface between durable memory and runtime truth
payload.summary: Stabilizing the Staccato Site showed that durable Site docs/config can lag or overstate runtime truth. We initially saw blocked-upstream text, but runtime status showed quiescent healthy state and confirmed outbound handoffs. Narada needs a governed site stabilization/reconciliation command or checklist.
payload.body:
## Observation

While stabilizing the Staccato client-service Site, durable Site memory and runtime truth disagreed.

Durable memory said:

```text
Willem task: blocked-upstream
outbound created: none
No email was sent
mailbox status: runtime_smoke_passed / later interpreted as blocked_missing_approval_effect_handoff
```

Runtime status showed:

```text
health: healthy
quiescent: true
active work: none
pending outbound handoffs: 0
confirmed outbound handoffs: 5
one Willem clarification draft terminal reason: sent_by_operator_request_via_graph
```

The correct durable posture after reconciliation was neither fully blocked nor fully complete. It was:

```text
operational_quiescent_with_approval_path_residual
```

## Learning

A Narada Site can have three different truth surfaces that need explicit reconciliation:

1. authored durable Site memory: tasks, chapters, KB, config;
2. runtime/control-plane state: work items, executions, outbound handoffs, health, quiescence;
3. operator/session knowledge: what was intentionally sent, deleted, superseded, or left as residual.

If these are reconciled manually, the architect can easily overcorrect: e.g. changing a Site from `runtime_smoke_passed` to `blocked` after seeing stale task text, even though runtime truth has advanced.

## Requested Capability

Narada should provide a governed Site stabilization/reconciliation surface, possibly:

```text
narada site stabilize
narada site reconcile-posture
narada operation readiness
narada mailbox readiness
```

The command/checklist should inspect:

- durable task/chapter/config posture;
- runtime health and quiescence;
- active/open/failed/retryable work items;
- pending/confirmed/terminal outbound handoffs;
- stale docs that contradict runtime truth;
- ignored runtime/private files that must not be committed;
- whether repo has a remote and whether publishing is expected;
- residuals that prevent full readiness.

It should produce a proposed posture update, not silently mutate doctrine or config.

## Additional Ergonomics

The current status output is useful but too raw for Site stabilization. It includes large JSON, confirmed draft payloads, and historical failed-terminal items. A bounded human summary would help:

- current health;
- quiescence;
- pending work;
- pending outbound/effects;
- latest confirmed effects;
- residuals;
- recommended durable posture.

This should avoid requiring the architect to infer readiness from raw control-plane output.

## Local Evidence

Staccato Site stabilization commit:

```text
2ae9814 Stabilize Staccato Narada site
```

Final local posture:

```text
operational_quiescent_with_approval_path_residual
```

Ignored local/private files remained out of Git:

```text
.narada/.env
.narada/state/
.narada/.ai/inbox.db
client artifacts
```

No remote was configured for the Staccato repo, so the durable Site was committed locally but not pushed.
evidence: Staccato durable task text said blocked-upstream while runtime status showed confirmed outbound handoffs
Manual reconciliation required reading git status, config, task docs, runtime status, and drafts output

promotion.note: recorded_pending_crossing: executable promotion for target kind 'decision' is not implemented yet.
